### —|Histoire de Paris|—|  ...    *(English translation)*, In 1190, before his departure for the third crusade, Philippe Auguste (1165-1223) asked the inhabitants to contribute to the security of the city by the construction of a wall of about 5 km in length, completed around 1210. This curtain wall, ten meters in height and crowned with a crenellated walkway, had a dozen gates. Bounded  ...    the king did not always reside there. This was the first attempt to unite the three Parisian districts: the "Cité" - the urban heart with religious, administrative and judiciary functions, the "Ville" - an economic hub located around ports on the right bank, and the nascent "University" on the left bank.. Before leaving for the crusade in 1190, Philip Augustus decided to protect the capital during his absence. He ordered Paris to close to a wall, long on the right bank of 2.6 km. The section located along the rue des Jardins Saint-Paul is now building even the largest visible in Paris. This wall is 120 meters, and measuring between 6 and 7 meters high. piste de l'enceinte médiévale Philippe-Auguste, rive droite (1/2)

Découvrir  ...    Entre 1190 et 1210, le jeune roi Philippe Auguste décide de faire construire une enceinte autour de Paris. Aujourd'hui  ...    particulier avec la ville où il aime séjourner lorsqu’il n’est pas en guerre contre les Anglais ou parti en croisade.  ...    Avant de devenir l'un des plus grands musées au monde, le Louvre était un château royal. Et avant d'être un château, c'était même une forteresse, construite entre 1190 et 1202 pour protéger Paris.
